stimulus: A first-generation Cuban American, Pedro Zamora ______blank as an HIV/AIDS educator (1990-93) and lectured at hundreds of high schools across the country when he joined the cast of MTV's The Real World: San Francisco for the 1994 season, becoming one of the first openly gay men with AIDS portrayed on American television.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. Zamora worked as an HIV/AIDS educator before he joined the cast of The Real World in 1994. To show that a past occurrence took place before another past occurrence, we need to use "had" + the past tense form of the verb. This is called the past perfect tense.

Choice B is incorrect. Zamora worked as an HIV/AIDS educator before he joined the cast. Both events are in the past, but his work as an educator happened earlier, so we need a verb that makes it clear that his work (and the lectures) had ended by the time he joined the cast. Choice C is incorrect. "Was already working" forms the continuous past tense, which we use when we're showing a past action that was ongoing. Both events are in the past, but they're not happening at the same time, so we shouldn't use the continuous past tense here. Choice D is incorrect. Zamora worked as an HIV/AIDS educator in the past, so we shouldn't use the present tense "works."
